chunks.text: The June issue of *IEEE Spectrum* is here! ### Topics ### Sections ### More ### For IEEE Members ### For IEEE Members ### IEEE Spectrum ### Follow IEEE Spectrum ### Support IEEE Spectrum ## Enjoy more free content and benefits by creating an account ## Saving articles to read later requires an IEEE Spectrum account ## The Institute content is only available for members ## Downloading full PDF issues is exclusive for IEEE Members ## Downloading this e-book is exclusive for IEEE Members ## Access to *Spectrum* 's Digital Edition is exclusive for IEEE Members ## Following topics is a feature exclusive for IEEE Members ## Adding your response to an article requires an IEEE Spectrum account ## Create an account to access more content and features on *IEEE Spectrum* , including the ability to save articles to read later, download Spectrum Collections, and participate in conversations with readers and editors. For more exclusive content and features, consider [Joining IEEE](/join) . ## Join the world’s largest professional organization devoted to engineering and applied sciences and get access to all of Spectrum’s articles, archives, PDF downloads, and other benefits. [Learn more about IEEE →](/join) ## Join the world’s largest professional organization devoted to engineering and applied sciences and get access to this e-book plus all of *IEEE Spectrum’s* articles, archives, PDF downloads, and other benefits. For full access and benefits, [subscribe](https://ieee.dragonforms.com/spectrum_subscribe) to *Spectrum*. # The Biggest Causes of Medical Device Recalls ## Software problems are responsible for 20 recalls a month Software problems are responsible for 20 recalls a month Stephen Cass is the special projects editor at IEEE Spectrum. ![An IV pump and drip apparatus in a hospital room with an LCD screen and keypad.](https://spectrum.ieee.org/media-library/an-iv-pump-and-drip-apparatus-in-a-hospital-room-with-an-lcd-screen-and-keypad.jpg?id=62219177&width=1200&height=1003) Medical devices frequently rely on embedded controllers and software. According to the [U.S. Food and Drug Administration](https://www.fda.gov/) records, in an average year over 2,500 [medical device recalls](https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cdrh/cfdocs/cfres/res.cfm) are issued in the [United States](https://spectrum.ieee.org/tag/united-states). Some of these recalls simply require checking the device for problems, but others require the return or destruction of the device. Once identified, the [FDA](https://spectrum.ieee.org/tag/fda) categorizes the root cause of these recalls into 40 categories, plus a catchall of “other”: situations that include labeling mix-ups, problems with expiration dates, and [counterfeiting](https://spectrum.ieee.org/tag/counterfeiting). ![chart visualization](https://public.flourish.studio/visualisation/26242465/thumbnail) What’s shown here is the breakdown of the five biggest problem categories found among the 56,000 entries in the FDA medical-recall database, which stretches back to 2002: device design, process control (meaning an error in the device’s manufacturing process), nonconforming material/component (meaning something does not meet required specifications), software issues, and packaging. ![chart visualization](https://public.flourish.studio/visualisation/26242199/thumbnail) [Software issues](https://spectrum.ieee.org/lean-software-development-2667603125) are broken down into six root causes, with software design far and away the biggest problem. The other five are, in order: change control; software design changes; software manufacturing or deployment problems; software design issues in the manufacturing process; and software in the “use environment.” That last one includes [cybersecurity](https://spectrum.ieee.org/tag/cybersecurity) issues, or

chunks.text: not meet required specifications), software issues, and packaging. ![chart visualization](https://public.flourish.studio/visualisation/26242199/thumbnail) [Software issues](https://spectrum.ieee.org/lean-software-development-2667603125) are broken down into six root causes, with software design far and away the biggest problem. The other five are, in order: change control; software design changes; software manufacturing or deployment problems; software design issues in the manufacturing process; and software in the “use environment.” That last one includes [cybersecurity](https://spectrum.ieee.org/tag/cybersecurity) issues, or problems with supporting software, such as a [smartphone app](https://spectrum.ieee.org/tag/smartphone-app). ![chart visualization](https://public.flourish.studio/visualisation/26242080/thumbnail) *This article appears in the December 2025 print issue as “Medical Device Recalls.”* [Stephen Cass](https://twitter.com/stephencass) is the special projects editor at *IEEE Spectrum*. He currently helms *Spectrum's* Hands On column, and is also responsible for interactive projects such as the [Top Programming Languages](/computing/software/the-2015-top-ten-programming-languages) app. He has a bachelor's degree in experimental physics from Trinity College Dublin. ![Ahmad Aljesirawi](https://spectrum.ieee.org/res/avatars/default "Ahmad Aljesirawi") The rise in medical-device recalls shows how vulnerable modern electronics are to radiation-induced bit flips and RF interference. Whether from solar flares or strong RF fields, a single corrupted “0” or “1” can crash life-critical software. As chips shrink, shielding, grounding, and robust EMC design aren’t optional anymore — they’re essential for patient safety. ![Several micro-satellites lined up in a lab setting.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 1200 800'%3E%3C/svg%3E) ## [Satellite IoT Contenders Are Racing Against a 6G Deadline](https://spectrum.ieee.org/satellite-iot-6g-lora-bluetooth) ![Utility workers inspect electrical equipment beside a service truck on a grassy site](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 1200 800'%3E%3C/svg%3E) ## [What It Takes for Future-Ready Power Distribution](https://spectrum.ieee.org/distribution-grid-modernization) ![Two photos of a prototype humanoid robot with a wooden frame and wires and other components strung on its body.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 1200 800'%3E%3C/svg%3E) ## [This DIY Bipedal Robot Used Pneumatic “Air-Muscles” Instead of Motors](https://spectrum.ieee.org/shadow-walker-biped-humanoid-robot) ## Related Stories ## [FDA Clarifies Wearable Device Rules](https://spectrum.ieee.org/fda-medical-device-rules) ## [Pressure-Relief Eye Tech Advances Toward Approval](https://spectrum.ieee.org/glaucoma-treatment-device) ## [Prototype Sensors Sniff Out Seizures Before They Occur](https://spectrum.ieee.org/epilepsy)
